connections.collecting_notes: Per-ChatGPT-account concurrency cap for Codex seats (card kt_1781998446396_02e627). All codex seats (gpt-5.5) share ONE OAuth account (~/.codex/auth.json, account 670497d4, direct backend); a big rally would 429 them all at once. seat-codex.sh acquires a lease here before exec codex (lease keyed to the codex PID -> auto-released on exit, crash-safe). connections.gaps_notes: 2026-07-14: service shows ActiveState=failed/ExecMainStatus=1 -- ROOT CAUSE (verified live via manual run + journalctl): this is NOT a script bug, the monitor is correctly detecting a REAL backup-freshness gap and exiting 1 by design -- ESTATE-BACKUP-RED SSD: latest snap 37.1h old (>26h threshold) | Drive: copy 37.1h old (>30h threshold) | verify: RESTORE-OK 5d ago (within its 8d threshold, ok). The monitor will self-clear to exit 0/RED-free once the underlying SSD-snapshot and Drive-copy cron jobs actually produce a fresh backup -- that is a separate fix (Henry noted a personal-backup fix already deployed/running elsewhere) and is NOT something this monitor script itself needs changed to satisfy.
connections.name: estate-backup-monitor
connections.quality_notes: Estate backup chain health monitor (SSD repo freshness <26h, Drive copy <30h, restore-verify <8d -> RED to cockpit Proposals). Unit: ~/.config/systemd/user/estate-backup-monitor.service + .timer. Script: /home/henry/bin/estate-backup-monitor. Registered 2026-07-14 close-drive (kt_1784028081100_240231).
connections.query_method: systemctl --user status estate-backup-monitor.service; journalctl --user -u estate-backup-monitor.service

connections.name: aero-attest
connections.quality_notes: W8 (kt_1783578413922_2f6584, 2026-07-10): verified-fact writer/reader for card-attestations.db. Source: ~/bin/aero-attest. DB: ~/work/factory/govern/card-attestations.db (overridable via AERO_ATTEST_DB). Design: henry-run writer records a verified fact; caged probesvc can sqlite3 -readonly to read it back. Filesystem enforces write-by-owner only (0644). Commands: record <card_id> <value> --by --evidence; get <card_id>; __probe_selftest. Derived-from-ledger path: some card_ids re-derive value from govern.db at read time (not stored value) for tamper-resistance.

connections.gaps_notes: Registry was stale for both this new job and the pre-existing aero-attest-crosshost cron job that runs every 30 minutes (a search for attest in scheduled jobs returned zero rows before this insert, despite aero-attest-crosshost running unregistered since T2, kt_1782898992267_47d7f9). Both jobs were de-staled in the same change per the move-equals-destale rule.
connections.name: aero-attest-runner
connections.quality_notes: attest-runner-builder-w20 2026-07-11 (mission: standing independent attestor, kt-attest-runner). Genuinely new identity distinct from aero-attest-crosshost (T2, hardcoded per-card declare-A maps, identity aero-attest-crosshost) -- this is the generic self-service extension: any card can request a standing attestation via a tag plus a sentinel block, with no code change to any script required. Proven live 2026-07-11 against kt_1783716806817_1eff82 (legal-backup card) via manual mode: ATTEST-PASS, an ssh personal tail of the run log showing RUN COMPLETE plus an ssh mac-mini hop to the QNAP listing both verified fresh, with the row landed in card-attestations.db under identity attest-runner.
connections.query_method: cron every 15 min on aerodeck (flock /tmp/aero-attest-runner.lock); scans :8649 kanban_list for tag attest:requested, parses ATTEST-REQUEST CHECK/EXPECT sentinels from the card description, executes read-only checks only (a write-verb denylist refuses any check resembling a delete, move, redirect, append, drop, insert, update, outward POST or PUT, permission change, kill, service restart, or destructive db write), writes card-attestations.db via aero-attest record with identity attest-runner, appends an INDEPENDENT-ATTEST progress_note to the card, and flips the tag to attest:done-pass or attest:done-fail. Capped at 10 cards per cycle.

connections.collecting_notes: AERFLOW: the manual headed-only pull queue for machinery/factory-repo cards. Henry law 2026-07-14: the machine can never modify its own machinery unattended. aero-dispatch's decide() SELF-FIX GUARD routes any card whose resolved repo is the factory itself (explicit repo:factory tag OR a BUILD-TARGET/probe path under /home/henry/work/factory) to SKIP reason=aerflow-headed-only and idempotently tags it queue:aerflow (mark_aerflow_queue_live) so it surfaces here. No unattended builder consumes this queue -- a human/headed seat pulls from it by hand.

connections.name: aero-verified-complete
connections.quality_notes: W8 (kt_1783578413922_2f6584, 2026-07-10): SK1 — the ONLY card closer. Source: ~/bin/aero-verified-complete. Blocking close gate: runs the card's own VERIFICATION-PROBE and checks against VERIFICATION-EXPECT regex. Card reaches done ONLY if probe output matches. Exit codes: 0=PASS(close), 2=board error, 3=no probe(fail-closed), 4=trivial/tautological probe(reject), 5=mismatch(refuse). Epic chain-verify: re-runs all child probes live before closing the parent. Board target: http://127.0.0.1:8649/mcp (overridable via AERO_KANBAN_MCP). Integrates aero-fable-judge (AERO_JUDGE=on) for verdict gating. Govern ledger: posts outcome event after successful close.

connections.quality_notes: claude oauth pool two-surface architecture note — Surface A (rotation): claude-pool-rotate + claude-pool-keepalive + aero-pool-cred-sync + aero-seat-rotator + aero-pool-doctor all running on aerodeck under systemd henry-user timers. connections.collecting_notes: Verified 2026-06-24: doctor green alive=5/5; seat tests PANE_OK + PERSONAL_OK. Source-of-truth: Company Brain record 13169 + infra memory note oauth-pool-architecture.
connections.name: claude-oauth-seat-pool
connections.quality_notes: Per-pane STATIC-token OAuth pool across aerodeck + personal (rebuilt 2026-06-24, replacing shared-profile+hot-swap). Each seat draws its own account from 5 static setup-tokens = parallel capacity (kills the all-seats-on-one rate-limit). ZERO refreshers anywhere, so the 2026-06-23 dual-refresh cascade is STRUCTURALLY impossible. cliproxy :8317 is Claude-free (Codex/Gemini only). Retired the cascade machinery (keepalive/sync/rotate-timer); backups in ~/bin/.retired-pool-20260624T113005Z + ~/pool-cleanup-backup-*.tar.gz. Limits: 5 accounts shared by aerodeck+personal+mally (capacity ceiling); running seats pinned to launch account (static tokens do not live-migrate, recycle to rebalance); ~yearly manual re-mint (doctor pre-warns).
connections.query_method: Per-host ~/.claude-pool/<acct>/ holds 5 static setup-tokens. claude-pool-pick assigns one account PER PANE by live quota (lease round-robin). Quota feed http://100.74.200.84:8930/api/swiftbar/oauth.json. aero-pool-doctor pages when live accounts < floor 2. Add an account: claude setup-token (browser) then claude-pool-add-oat <email>.

connections.collecting_notes: Registered 2026-07-16 (T8, card kt_1784158784989_18eb4b, Henry infra leg). systemd act-workspaces-bridge.service, User=henry, EnvironmentFile=/etc/act-workspaces-bridge.env (root:root 600, ACT_WORKSPACES_BEARER sealed via the fleet secret pipeline: secret-get act-workspaces-bridge-bearer). Narrow-scope wrapper around the EXISTING tmux-bridge (:8282 full / :8283 biz-scoped) substrate -- does NOT reimplement tmux control, calls tmux capture-pane/send-keys/list-panes directly for a curated allowlist (allowlist.json: fable:0.0 Mission Control + business:0.0/0.1 + biz:0.0/0.1 operator panes). Serves the Act Workspaces tab (/agents/act, DECISION.md T8/V6) machinery: GET /status (all-panes label/window/title/last-line, unrestricted enumeration), GET /stream?pane=<target> (SSE live tail, allowlist-gated), POST /send (allowlist-gated + steer.py-identical DESTRUCTIVE structural refusal + kill-switch check + durable sqlite audit row BEFORE the tmux call returns). Bearer auth fail-CLOSED on every route (unlike tmux-bridge sibling fail-open default) -- curl with no/bad token returns 401 always, verified live 2026-07-16. Distinct from AeroCockpit (:8666, aerocockpit-web row) which is the broader estate-wide owner console; this is the dedicated audited machinery for one Act tab.
connections.name: act-workspaces-bridge
connections.quality_notes: Kill switch: /home/henry/work/infra/act-workspaces-bridge/killswitch.sh {on|off|status} -- flag file data/KILL_SWITCH checked on every /send; toggle fires a gates-bot :8688 /notify (verified live 2026-07-16, notify_hold rows 4000/4001). Audit ledger: sqlite data/audit-ledger.db table sends(ts,who,target,keys,result), one row per send attempt incl. refusals, durable across restarts. Destructive-shaped refusal imports voice-brain/tools/steer.py DESTRUCTIVE regex directly (no re-derivation). | WAR-GAME 2026-07-16 (independent adversary session, agent accd1b3f89da9adb1): MISS=11 on the destructive-refusal blast surface (spaced-letter split, $IFS reassembly, fork bomb, fullwidth-unicode homoglyph, base64/hex decode-piped-to-shell x3, ANSI-escape smuggling, quote-concatenation, variable reassembly, non-string keys type-confusion) + 1 allowlist byte-identity gap (trailing-whitespace variants of an allowlisted name were accepted). CONFIRMED LIVE-FIRE: the 11 payloads were submitted with Enter into the live fable:0.0 (Mission Control) pane during the war-game (the bridge always presses Enter; a dry-run flag did not exist at war-game time) -- Mission Control (a live Opus 4.8 agent) recognised every payload as destructive-shaped on its own judgment and executed nothing; no filesystem/process damage occurred (independently verified post-incident). FIXED same session: is_destructive() hardening layer (NFKC unicode fold, ANSI-strip, quote-strip, $IFS-normalize, fork-bomb pattern fixed [steer.py upstream \b bug flagged separately], pipe-to-shell-interpreter rule, variable-concat-into-flag rule, whitespace-stripped tight-fragment fallback) + strict isinstance(str) type check on target/keys (400 on type mismatch) + allowlist exact-match (no .strip()). Permanent regression: python3 server.py --selftest -> SELFTEST-OK (14 checks, 0 failures), re-verified live via HTTP against all 4 sharpest repros post-restart -> all now 403/400 (was 200). server.py.bak-pre-wargame-hardening-20260716T0030Z kept for rollback.

connections.name: aeros-runner-1
connections.quality_notes: Original/first GitHub Actions self-hosted runner on aeros (pre-existing, backfilled into the registry 2026-07-14 -- the a27d7ced deploy-runner-wedge-fix report flagged this row as a registry gap: aeros-runner-2 was registered 09:07Z but the sibling runner-1 never was). Org-level (gitHubUrl https://github.com/aerodeck-ai), gh api orgs/aerodeck-ai/actions/runners id=11, status online. systemd unit actions.runner.aerodeck-ai.aeros-runner-1.service, User=ubuntu, WorkingDirectory=/mnt/data/actions-runner. version 2.335.1. Labels as of 2026-07-14T16:xx (post PR #1325 + stray-ci-gates-label cleanup at 15:54Z): self-hosted,Linux,ARM64,deploy-aeros-prod,deploy-aeros-dev -- the ONLY runner carrying deploy-aeros-prod (deploy-prod.yml, deploy-dev.yml, runner-smoke.yml, prod-drift-tripwire.yml all pin to it exclusively). Also carries deploy-aeros-dev so it can pick up deploy-dev-design-aeros.yml when runner-2 is busy. Shares /mnt/data/buildx-cache/aeros-os with runner-2 (deliberate warm-cache handoff between the dev-design mirror leg and the prod-promote leg); PR #1330 (fix/aeros-buildx-cache-lock, 2026-07-14) added an flock(/mnt/data/buildx-cache/aeros-os.lock) critical section around both legs' docker buildx build + cache-export + prune to stop the concurrent-writer "context canceled" export race that a second runner made newly possible (root-caused against run 29346372970, 15:51:52Z 2026-07-14).

connections.name: aeros-runner-2
connections.quality_notes: Second GitHub Actions self-hosted runner on aeros, installed 2026-07-14 (card kt_1784019726119_744d95, Henry go-on-all 2026-07-14) to stop PR-gate/deploy serialization through the sole aeros-runner-1. Org-level (gitHubUrl https://github.com/aerodeck-ai), version 2.335.1 (matches runner-1), systemd unit actions.runner.aerodeck-ai.aeros-runner-2.service, User=ubuntu, WorkingDirectory=/mnt/data/actions-runner-2. LABELS DE-STALED 2026-07-14T16:xx (this note was stale -- originally installed with labels self-hosted,Linux,ARM64,aeros and NO deploy label; a same-day relabel to the deploy-aeros-prod/deploy-aeros-dev function-label scheme + a stray ci-gates label picked up along the way was REMOVED at 15:54Z per agent a27d7ced/PR-era cleanup, backup /tmp/runner-label-bak/aeros-runner-2-labels-BEFORE-20260714T1554Z.json). CURRENT live labels (verified via gh api orgs/aerodeck-ai/actions/runners 2026-07-14T16:3x): self-hosted,Linux,ARM64,deploy-aeros-dev -- carries deploy-aeros-dev ONLY, so deploy-dev-design-aeros.yml can land here concurrently with a deploy-prod.yml run on runner-1 (which alone carries deploy-aeros-prod); prod deploys never schedule onto runner-2. This is exactly the concurrency the second runner exists for (proof: run 29320672428 deploy-prod on runner-1 concurrent with run 29320797794 deploy-dev-design-aeros on runner-2, both busy=true simultaneously, 2026-07-14 09:11-09:12Z). Shared /mnt/data/buildx-cache/aeros-os cache dir with runner-1 (not partitioned, deliberate warm-cache handoff) -- PR #1330 (fix/aeros-buildx-cache-lock) added flock(/mnt/data/buildx-cache/aeros-os.lock) around both legs' cache-touching build+export+prune section to stop the concurrent-writer export race the label change made newly reachable (root-caused against the 15:51:52Z "context canceled: error writing layer blob" failure on run 29346372970).

Pre-change snapshot: personal:/home/ubuntu/iptables-pre-wa3001-20260714.bak (341 lines).
